section: Chapter: When People Rebel – 1857 and After

questions.question: When did the Revolt of 1857 begin?
questions.explanation: The revolt started in 1857 with a sepoy uprising in Meerut.

questions.question: Where did the Revolt of 1857 start?
questions.explanation: Sepoys in Meerut rebelled against the British and marched to Delhi.

questions.question: Who were the sepoys?
questions.correct_answer: Indian soldiers in the British army.
questions.explanation: They played a major role in starting the revolt.

questions.question: Who was the Mughal emperor during the revolt?
questions.explanation: The rebels declared him the leader of the revolt.

questions.question: What was one immediate cause of the revolt?
questions.explanation: Sepoys believed the cartridges were greased with cow and pig fat.

questions.question: Who led the revolt in Jhansi?
questions.explanation: She fought bravely against the British during the revolt.

questions.question: Who led the revolt in Kanpur?
questions.explanation: He was one of the important leaders of the rebellion.

questions.question: Who led the revolt in Lucknow?
questions.explanation: She organized resistance against the British in Lucknow.

questions.question: What happened to Bahadur Shah Zafar after the revolt?
questions.explanation: The British removed him from power and sent him to Burma.

questions.question: What major change happened after the revolt of 1857?
questions.correct_answer: India came under direct rule of the British Crown.
questions.explanation: The East India Company’s rule ended in 1858.

notes.title: Revolt of 1857
notes.content: The Revolt of 1857 was the first large-scale rebellion against British rule in India.

notes.title: Causes of the Revolt
notes.content: Major causes included political annexations, economic exploitation and the use of greased cartridges.

notes.title: Leaders of the Revolt
notes.content: Important leaders included Rani Lakshmibai, Nana Sahib, Begum Hazrat Mahal and Kunwar Singh.

notes.title: Role of Sepoys
notes.content: Indian soldiers in the British army started the revolt in Meerut and spread it to other regions.

notes.title: Aftermath of the Revolt
notes.content: After the revolt, the British Crown took direct control of India and the East India Company rule ended.

facts.fact: The Revolt of 1857 started in Meerut.
Bahadur Shah Zafar was the last Mughal emperor.
The East India Company lost control of India after 1858.
The revolt involved sepoys, peasants, rulers and common people.
